context.description: A philosophical chapter section titled "Gewissheit" unfolds as an allegorical story about a hardworking man who sacrifices the present for promised freedom and fulfillment. The narrative turns to doubt when he sees that the shared burden may be a hellish illusion of suffering, death, and chained labor. He briefly returns to the old burden, reinforcing the tension between certainty and awakening.
normalization_notes: Source text is German; human-readable values translated to English.
No explicit chapter number or section title is shown; chapter metadata left null.

rows.paragraph_text: An allegorical philosophical passage about a righteous man who endures labor in hopes of freedom and fulfillment.
His certainty erodes as years of sacrifice deepen the burden and he begins to doubt whether the promised goal can be reached.
A horrifying vision reveals chained sufferers, disease, death, and a hellish landscape before he briefly returns to his former burden.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2, section heading Certainty. Philosophical allegory about sacrifice, certainty, doubt, and a hellish revelation of suffering and chained labor.
